title: Protecting our Communities from Sexual Predators Act
short_title: Protecting our Communities from Sexual Predators Act
plain_summary: This bill makes new rules about non-citizens who commit sexual assault crimes in America. If a non-citizen is arrested or charged with sexual assault, they must be held in jail and cannot be released. Non-citizens who are convicted of sexual assault cannot enter the United States and must be removed from the country if they are already here. The bill is meant to protect communities by making sure sexual predators who are not American citizens are kept away from the public.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting our Communities from Sexual Predators Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Detention of Certain Aliens Who Commit Sexual Assault
sections.text: This section changes the Immigration and Nationality Act to add a new rule about holding certain people in detention. The law will now require that people who are not US citizens must be held in detention if they are not allowed to enter the country under certain immigration rules and they have been charged with, arrested for, found guilty of, or have admitted to committing sexual assault. Sexual assault is defined the same way as it is defined elsewhere in immigration law.

sections.title: Section 3 Inadmissilibity and Deportability Related to Sexual Assault
sections.text: This section makes two major changes to immigration law about sexual assault. First, it adds a new rule that people who are not US citizens cannot be allowed to enter the United States if they have been found guilty of sexual assault, have admitted to committing sexual assault, or have admitted to doing things that are basically sexual assault. Second, it adds a new rule that people who are not US citizens can be removed from the United States if they have been found guilty of sexual assault, have admitted to committing sexual assault, or have admitted to doing things that are basically sexual assault. In both cases, sexual assault is defined the same way as it is defined elsewhere in immigration law.

last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
